entry.title: Get Started with SAP BTP Document Management Service (video)
entry.content:
Many of you may have been introduced to the SAP BTP Document Management Service in using SAP Build process Automation, which has some integrations with DMS so your process can allow users to upload documents and you can add those documents to approval forms.

DMS also provides other capabilities, such as versioning, search, and access control, and can be used as a content server from the SAP S/4HANA attachment service. @AlexDong has written a wonderful blog collecting a lot of the information about DMS, especially from an SAP S/4HANA perspective.

To help, especially for newbies who've seen DMS from SAP Build perspective, I have created a TechByte video to show what DMS is, the different parts of DMS, how to install the Integration (API) Option, and how to make API calls to work with repos and documents.

In future videos, we'll look at the application option for doing the same thing but with a GUI, how to access documents on mobile, and finally how to work with DMS in SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps.

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter August 2024
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Please note: A one-time registration for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support [accessible here] is necessary to register for these events.

 

SAP Datasphere Fundamentals

Boost your SAP Datasphere project by joining one of our new Expert-guided Implementation virtual workshops. Our aim is that through the course of these enablement sessions we help you understand key concepts and see a quicker time to value when adopting SAP Datasphere.

Details and dates in this blog post

Introduction of SAP Cloud ALM

Are you new to SAP Cloud ALM, looking to educate yourself about this topic? Gain a first understanding of what SAP Cloud ALM offers, understand its significance, and explore ways to onboard. How? We have a great recording of our recent virtual expert session for you.

Access here: Introduction of SAP Cloud ALM

E-Learning: SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise Planning

Take our new E-learning, which is designed to guide you through a full interactive overview on Enterprise Planning with SAP Analytics Cloud. This comes with a sample dataset included so that you can follow along in your own tenant at your own pace.

Access through this blog: SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise Planning

Digital Badges - Show Your Digital Skills to the World!

Did you know we currently offer six learning programs that include an official SAP digital badge? You can earn these badges after successful completion of the specific learning program and share within your network as proof of achievement.

Take the chance and earn your badge today!

Guiding You to Achieve a Clean Core

Enterprise Support. Get started by visiting SAP Enterprise Support value maps. Included with every SAP Cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can navigate to curated content, be directed to self-service platforms, and engage with peers and SAP experts. Join today for quick access to a wealth of value acceleration content!

Watch the video

Enhanced Continuous Quality Check for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network

Planning an SAP Ariba go-live? Adding solutions to your existing SAP Ariba suite? Need a “health check” for your productive SAP Ariba or SAP Business Network system? Our enhanced continuous quality check from SAP Enterprise Support has you covered.

Learn more

SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council Q3 Update

Join the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) program with our new and open workstreams for Q3, covering all major SAP Cloud solutions. Benefit from collaboration, innovation, and gain early access to new tools and resources.

Further details

Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Finance – Deep Dive Sessions

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about all phases and activities involved in Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA. We have new sessions open in August and September, available in Chinese and English language.

More in this blog post

How to Get Started With SAP S/4HANA Finance

Are you about to start with SAP S/4HANA Finance? Then don’t miss our interactive virtual Mee-the-Expert live sessions upcoming in September that help you get on board.

Register through this blog post: Get started with SAP S/4HANA Finance

SAP S/4HANA Finance Fundamentals–Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about delta capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Finance. We have two sessions in August, available in English and Chinese language.

More in this blog post

Watch the Replays of Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

We hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We’ve got you covered! Watch the replays on demand.

The Eastland Group leverages SAP Cloud ALM to gain enhanced understanding of system performance, proactively identify potential issues, and perform detailed analysis through user-friendly dashboards.

Read the full quote: sap.to/61109kBuxt

Full details

AA Industrial Belting was impressed by the thorough and informative guidance provided by the SAP product support and development teams regarding the features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including legal reporting, sales analytics, and credit management. The Real-Time Support approach allowed for quick understanding and effective communication, which helped AA Industrial Belting learn about the product features efficiently.

entry.title: Statement of Direction – SAP Information Steward
entry.content:
August 2024

Introduction

As our matured products continue to have consistent traction in the market with emerging technologies and data platforms, it is essential for us to focus on extending its versions to meet the evolving needs of our customers. This Statement of Direction outlines our strategic approach to extending product versions, ensuring that we maintain our competitive edge in the on-premises data stewardship, data quality, metadata, and glossary space as well as on-cloud and deliver value to our customers. Our primary objective is to understand and address the specific requirements of our customers, for the matured products as well as new and emerging technologies in the cloud. We will actively engage with our user base through feedback sessions, support channels, and market research to identify their pain points, challenges, and emerging needs.

 

Executive Summary

This document provides guidance to our customers of SAP Data Management portfolio, with respect to SAP Information Steward (IS) solution, on the product directions.  For Data Services (DS) we have a separate blog communication made available. This document is an updated version of our blog post made on November 3, 2023, which focused on SAP Information Steward and SAP Data Services.

SAP Data Management portfolio expanded further in the cloud with the introduction of SAP Datasphere in March 2023. SAP Datasphere is a comprehensive data service built only on SAP® Business Technology platform (SAP BTP) that enables every data professional to deliver seamless and scalable access to mission critical business data. It is the next generation of SAP® Data Warehouse Cloud, with new capabilities that enhance data discovery, data catalog, metadata, modeling, and distribution. With a unified experience for data cataloging, semantic modeling, data integration, data warehousing, data federation, and data virtualization.  SAP Datasphere enables data professionals to easily distribute authoritative business data with business context and logic preserved across the data landscape.

As we define the future of SAP Data Management portfolio, with our stated data management strategy, current on-premise SAP Information Steward customers can leverage their existing investments and efforts, repurposing those time-tested and trusted artifacts and configurations that are being used today in SAP Information Steward (IS), while also extending the reach of their data management to the cloud, with SAP Datasphere, a native cloud solution. We are committed to our existing SAP Information Steward and customer base too.

In 2022 we did a minor version upgrade of SAP Information Steward to 4.3, allowing the compatibility with Business Intelligence BusinessObjects 4.3. As a result, our support policy extended the end of mainstream maintenance timeline to end of 2025 and priority 1 support to end of 2027 for both SAP Information Steward and SAP Data Services. However, with the mainstream maintenance of version 4.3 of SAP Information Steward ending in 2025, we do not want to leave our large customer base behind, and therefore we have decided to release an updated version, code named SAP Information Steward 2025 to provide more time to our on-premises customers to adopt our rapidly evolving, state-of-the-art cloud data quality and data governance scenarios. Many of our largest SAP Information Steward customers already run both solutions side-by-side, during their transition to the managed cloud deployment.

 

SAP Information Steward 2025

Our current plan to release a new version of the SAP Information Steward in 2025 as a successor of SAP Information Steward 4.3 remains unchanged and will be...

  • Code named SAP Information Steward 2025
  • Available on-premises
  • Focused on the most widely adopted on-premises scenarios: SAP Information Steward for data stewardship, metadata, glossary, and data quality
  • Compatible with proposed BI 2025 suite and planned to be released a quarter after BI 2025
  • We have plans to release the first version by the end of Q2 2025 followed by ‘Fix Packages’ as needed.
  • We have plans to release a new version every alternate year and reduce the frequency of SAP Information Steward, (SP) Service Pack releases. The ‘Service Pack’ release has been planned to be deprecated.
  • Continue to support Windows and Linux platforms only.
  • We do not foresee any impact to solutions and partners using SAP Information Steward in an OEM mode.   

We are committed to support you.

We recognize the importance of continuously improving and innovating our product and yet manage a balance between our on-premises and cloud-based solutions. We will invest in research and development to explore new technologies, industry trends, and market demands yet support our existing customer base for the mature products. By fostering a culture of innovation and collaboration, we will ensure that our product versions remain cutting-edge and relevant in the ever-changing business landscape. We are committed to help and support our customers.

Many customers rely on SAP Information Steward for critical use cases and will need to do so beyond 2025. Our support plan for SAP Information Steward is as follows and our message is consistent. Please refer to the respective GA versions Product Availability Matrix for the support timelines.

 

WE WANT TO HEAR FROM YOU

SAP Information Steward remains as a key component of our Data Management portfolio, and we are committed to our customers. Please help us fine tune our roadmaps of SAP Information Steward providing your input through the dedicated inbox:

SAP Information Steward:               sap.informationsteward.enhance@sap.com

We appreciate your feedback and will answer your questions via our official communication channels.

entry.title: What is SAP Approuter and its importance ?
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Approuter is a lightweight service, which acts as a single point of entry for various back-end services and applications in SAP ecosystem. In many SAP cloud foundry enterprise applications, Approuter plays an essential role in tying together different pieces of the application landscape and ensuring smooth user experience. In this blog, I am going to share my learning on Approuter and its usage.

What is SAP Approuter

SAP Approuter is a Node.js library used as a single point-of-entry to an enterprise application. The Approuter is used to serve static content, authenticate users, rewrite URLs, and forward or proxy requests to other micro services while propagating user information.

Which environments Approuter can be used in

  • SAP Cloud Foundry, SAP BTP
  • SAP XSA – XS Advanced (On Premise)
  • Kyma Runtime (Standalone Approuter)

Types of Approuter available in SAP BTP

There are two types of Approuter available in SAP BTP, which can be used for the applications deployed in SAP Cloud Foundry.

  • Standalone Approuter :
    • Standalone Approuter needs to be setup and run by application owner, it allows customization/extension as per application need.  
  • Managed Approuter :
    • Managed Approuter is provided by SAP, so application owner need not to maintain own runtime infrastructure.
    • The Managed Approuter is the HTML5 applications runtime capability that is provided by the following products. To use the managed application router, we must be subscribed to one of these services.
      • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
      • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
      • SAP Cloud Portal
    • SAP recommends to use Managed Approuter if there is no extension required.

Primary features and functionalities of SAP Approuter

  • Request Routing: The Approuter can route incoming requests to different microservices, or applications based on the URL pattern.
  • Authentication and Authorization: The Approuter is tightly integrated with the SAP Cloud Platform’s authentication and authorization mechanisms. It can facilitate user authentication via various methods like SAML, and then manage user sessions.
  • Destination Resolution: In the SAP Cloud environment, a “destination” is a predefined set of configurations that point to a specific backend system or service. The Approuter can resolve these destinations and direct the requests appropriately.
  • Service-to-Service Calls: Approuter supports scenarios where services need to communicate with each other without the need for user intervention.
  • Serve Static Content: It can also be configured to serve static content, such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files.
  • Integration with XSUAA: SAP’s User Account and Authentication (XSUAA) service is commonly used for authentication and authorization in the SAP Cloud Platform, and Approuter is well-integrated with it.
  • Extension and Customization: The Approuter is built on Node.js, which means that developers can extend its capabilities or integrate it with other Node.js modules if needed.

Approuter project structure and it’s components.

<myApproute>

  |- xs-app.json               # Application routes configuration

  |- package.json              # Application router details/dependencies

 

 

 

Architecture diagram of an application

Suppose we have a requirement to develop a Project Management solution for one of the organizations having SAP BTP. The solution needs to integrate with SAP SuccessFactors for employee’s HR related information and needs to integrate with SAP On-premises system for other information. In this case we can develop an SAP UI5 based application for user interface and APIs in Java Spring boot / node JS to save or read data on HANA Cloud DB and to interact with APIs/OData services from SAP and SuccessFactors system. The APIs will be available publicly as this is hosted in SAP BTP, so it is very important to securely expose the APIs by using SAP BTP’s authentication and authorization service, which can be easily done by using SAP Approuter. Here is the architecture of the application.

 

Thanks,

Sudhir.

entry.title: Leveraging Image Embedding and HANA Vector Engine for Reverse Product Search
entry.content:
Introduction

HANA Cloud Vector Engine significantly accelerates the development of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) applications. Traditionally, RAG solutions have focused on leveraging text data from internal documents. However, with the rise of multimodal AI, as seen with GPT-4o, there is a growing trend towards accepting various input types such as images, audio, and video. To maximize the power of Vector Engine, integrating image similarity search is essential for enhancing its capabilities.

Reference: HANA Database Vector Engine Guide

Use Cases

Imagine a potential customer visiting a friend’s house and discovering an amazing chair. However, his friend can’t remember where he bought it. This is where Reverse Product Search comes into play. The customer takes a picture of the chair and finds that your company sells the exact same model. Reverse Product Search is particularly useful when customers cannot identify a product by name or barcode. It’s also ideal for finding repair parts or similar items.

Image Similarity Search with HANA Vector Engine

Embedding Product Images

The first step is to convert images into num erical data so that a computer can measure the similarity between them. In this prototype, we used OpenAI's CLIP, a zero-shot model that links images and text for cross-modal understanding without task-specific training.

 

from langchain_experimental.open_clip import OpenCLIPEmbeddings

clip_embd = OpenCLIPEmbeddings(model_name="ViT-g-14", checkpoint="laion2b_s34b_b88k")

 

This code embeds the product images and stores them in HANA Cloud.

Product images are preloaded into image_storage using the Product Master API.

 

def process_images(clip_embd, hana_conn, image_storage):
    
    cursor = hana_conn.cursor()
    images = image_storage.get_images()

    csv_rows = []
    for i, img in enumerate(images):
        model = 'OpenCLIP'
        embedding =  clip_embd.embed_image([BytesIO(img["data"])])
        csv_rows.append([i,img["name"],model,str(embedding[0])])

    sql = '''
      INSERT INTO EMBEDDING_CONTENT ("id","content","model","EMBEDDING")
      VALUES (?,?,?,TO_REAL_VECTOR(?))
    '''
    
    try:
        cursor.executemany(sql, csv_rows) 
    except Exception as e:
        hana_conn.rollback()
        print("An error occurred:", e)        
    finally:
        cursor.close()

 

Retrieving Similar Images

Once the product images are embedded and stored in HANA Cloud, you can retrieve images similar to a photo taken by a smartphone. Specifically, this code embeds the mobile_image and compares the cosine similarity between the mobile_image and each image in HANA Cloud. Finally, it retrieves and ranks the top 5 images based on their similarity scores.

 

def process_mobile_image(clip_embd, hana_conn, mobile_image):
    
    cursor = hana_conn.cursor() 
    embedding =  clip_embd.embed_image([BytesIO(mobile_image)])
    
    sql = '''
      SELECT TOP 5 "id"
      FROM "EMBEDDING_CONTENT"
      ORDER BY COSINE_SIMILARITY("EMBEDDING", TO_REAL_VECTOR(?)) DESC
    '''
    
    try:
        cursor.execute(sql, (str(embedding[0]),))
        retrieved_products = cursor.fetchall()
    except Exception as e:
        hana_conn.rollback()
        print("An error occurred:", e)        
    finally: 
        cursor.close()
        return retrieved_products

 

Reverse Product Search with BTP SDK for iOS

Interacting with Product Master API

Product images are managed as attachments of product master data so that they can be easily accessed by application.
To download attachments, you have to use Get All Originals operation to retrieve the metadata of the attachment.
Reference: Get All Originals

 

GET <host>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/GetAllOriginals?LinkedSAPObjectKey='TestEKKO'&BusinessObjectTypeName='EKKO_RFQ'


Using the metadata retrieved, you can download an attachment from the product master data.
Reference: Download Attachment Content

 

GET<host>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet(DocumentInfoRecordDocType='SL1',DocumentInfoRecordDocNumber='10000002076',DocumentInfoRecordDocPart='000',DocumentInfoRecordDocVersion='00',LogicalDocument='1C98EC1818551EDA998508E3A05E0BCE',ArchiveDocumentID='1C98EC1818551EDA998508E3A05E4BCE',LinkedSAPObjectKey='TESTEKKO',BusinessObjectTypeName='EKKO_RFQ')/$value

 

BTP SDK for iOS

To build an end-to-end solution, I have developed an iOS application using the BTP SDK for iOS. This application captures images, sends them to a server-side application, and displays a ranking of similar products.

 

Conclusion

As we have explored, integrating Image Similarity Search unveils new potential for HANA Vector Engine. Additionally, seamless integration with SAP technologies such as S/4HANA Product Master and BTP SDK for iOS enables the efficient development of sophisticated and innovative enterprise applications.

 

References

Enhancing S/4HANA with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Store and GenAI

First steps using the Hana Vector Engine with SAP GEN AI

Mastering SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine for RAG-driven LLM Applications: An Insightful Guide

entry.title: Generative AI with SAP – Part 2: Components of SAP Business AI
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Generative AI with SAP, which is focused on understanding how SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across its portfolio.

In this series we are learning:

  • How SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across it’s portfolio.
  • What SAP Business AI is and how SAP partners and customers can use it?
  • What is SAP AI Core? What is SAP AI launchpad? What is Joule? etc.
  • What role SAP BTP plays in SAP's AI strategy? ... and many more.

 

To make it easy to understand, the blog series is divided in small parts. Each blog requires maximum 10–15 minutes to learn.

 

  • Part 1 – Introduction to SAP Business AI 
  • Part 2 – Components of SAP Business AI [Current blog]
  • Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot
  • Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP 
  • Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core
  • Part 6 – Introduction to SAP AI launchpad
  • Part 7 – Get Started with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad
  • part 8 - Hello World using SAP AI Core
  • Part 9 – Overview of Generative AI Hub
  • Part 10 – SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
  • Part 11 - RAG, a real-life use case using SAP AI Core, Generative AI Hub and HANA Vector Engine

 

This is the 2nd blog in the series, where will learn what are major components of SAP Business AI.

Note: I will publish the subsequent  blogs soon.

 

Components of SAP Business AI

Major components of SAP Business AI are:

  • Joule
  • Embedded AI Applications
  • AI Foundation

Below image gives a high level overview of each component.

 

 

Now, let’s take a close look into these components.

 

Joule

Joule is:

  • An advanced, generative AI copilot
  • embedded throughout SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio
  • To deliver proactive and contextualized insights from across the breadth and depth of SAP’s applications and third-party sources

Joule is designed to helps people get work done faster and drive better business outcomes in a secure, compliant way.

Below summarizes major points about Joule.

 

For more information on Joule, refer to Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

 

Embedded AI Capabilities

Under the umbrella of SAP Business AI, SAP is embedding the capabilities of AI across entire SAP solutions. These AI-powered capabilities include matching, intelligent business document processing, personalized recommendations, natural language processing, forecasting and predictions, and many more.

SAP is offering these embedded AI capabilities across all business areas including finance, supply chain, procurement, human resources, sales, marketing, and IT.

Here are some examples:

In Finance, SAP Cash Application allow customers to automate invoice matching. It automates and simplify the order-to-cash process in accounts receivable accounting.

In Supply Chain, SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain allow customers to predict demand reliably with AI-powered demand forecasting.

In Human Resources, Personalized Recommendation, one of the SAP AI Business Services embedded in SAP SuccessFactors enable customers to improve the employee experience at scale.

You may refer to this page to know more about how customers can benefit from AI built into the core business processes — connecting finance, supply chain, procurement, sales, marketing, human resources, and IT as shown in image below.

 

AI Foundation on SAP BTP

AI foundation on SAP BTP is a set of ready-to-use AI services and tools, which:

  • enable customers and partners
  • to create AI-powered extensions and applications
  • in a reliable, secure, and scalable way

 

Below image gives a high-level picture of AI foundation.

 

 Note: For more information on AI foundation, refer to Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP [to be published]

 

SAP’s Partnership with AI Vendors to Provide an Abstraction for SAP Customers

SAP has partnered with leading general-purpose AI vendors and large language model (LLM) providers to ensure that SAP customers are able to reap the benefit of AI innovations without going into individual contract with 3rd party vendors. SAP has made several strategic partnerships with Google, IBM, Nvidia, Microsoft and others.

For example:

SAP is leveraging Microsoft's Azure OpenAI Service to provide you access to powerful LLMs like GPT-3 without any 3rd party contract. From Generative AI Hub, you can access these large language models from multiple vendors easily.

SAP has partnered with NVIDIA to leverage its AI hardware and software solutions, for example NVIDIA V100 Tensor Core GPU can be used as one of the underlying infrastructures for SAP AI Core.  

 

Three Core Principles of SAP Business AI

SAP Business AI is built with three core principles: relevant, reliable, and responsible.

Relevant

In general, "relevant" means that AI systems should provide responses which are perfectly aligned with the user's current needs and context.

For example, if you ask an AI virtual assistant – “Find me a good restaurant to go now”, it should give the list of restaurants best on your specific food preferences and the current time. If it’s a dinner time, and the virtual assistant suggest a breakfast café OR if you are a vegetarian and it suggests a non-vegetarian restaurant, then it’s not Relevant for you.

Similarly, SAP Business AI understand the business context to become more relevant for customers and end users.

SAP Business AI is relevant from day one since it is built in across all SAP applications that customers already use to run their most critical business processes. These embedded AI capabilities are designed keeping business context in mind.

 

Reliable

In general, "reliable" refers to the consistency, accuracy, and dependability of the AI system in performing its tasks and producing the desired outcomes.

For example, a predictive AI model is considered reliable only if consistently predicts outcomes accurately, whether it's for stock prices, weather forecasting, or patient diagnoses. Or a chatbot is considered reliable only if understands user queries correctly and provides accurate and helpful responses consistently.

Reliability is one of the main core principles of SAP Business AI.

The services and solutions under SAP Business AI are built on rigorous product standards, and enterprise-readiness that customers expect from SAP. These AI applications are trained on industry and business process data, making it more reliable for customers.

Customers can trust the SAP Business AI to perform its intended function accurately, consistently, and dependably.

 

Responsible

In general, AI system being "responsible" refers to ensuring that AI systems are developed, deployed, and used in a manner that aligns with ethical principles, respects human rights, promotes fairness, and mitigates potential risks or harms.

SAP is deeply committed to AI ethics in line with our core values. SAP Business AI is designed for people to get their best work done, valuing human oversight and agency.

SAP system contains business critical and confidential data. SAP customers trust SAP with their mission-critical business processes and most sensitive data. Therefore, SAP AI applications and services are built using responsible AI. SAP uses a robust ethical framework that guides the development and operations of AI.

 

What’s Next?

Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

 

Disclaimer – All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2408?
entry.content:
As you may know by now, SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition is a software-as-a-service (Saas) solution on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that is used to govern core attributes of business partner data (more domains planned). This public cloud solution is perfectly suited for organizations seeking at kick-starting a master data management initiative at low entry barriers (see also the overview presentation). 

Full Functional Scope

For details of the complete functional scope, see the latest deep-dive presentation about SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition.

What's New?

Continuing the path of innovations outlined in my blog post on the 2405 release, the 2408 version comes with the following enhancements:

Enhancements to the core attributes of the business partner data model

You can now assign one or multiple organizational units such as purchasing organization, sales area, or company code to a business partner. This feature comprises the assignment of organizational units only. It does not include further organizational attributes like reconciliation accounts, incoterms, or payment methods, etc. Features include:

  • Option to create an organizational unit, e.g. purchasing organization, sales area, or company code, directly when creating a business partner using the quick create view or the object page​
  • Assignment of multiple organizational units
  • At least one supplier or customer role is required to create a supplier or customer organizational unit.​
  • All MDG processes support the organizational unit assignment.​
  • Possibility to authorize users to create and/or display organizational units
  • Possibility to upload the business configuration from the backend system to SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition via spreadsheet
  • Possibility to define validation rules for organizational units

Fig.: Assignment of organizational units to supplier record

You can exchange organizational units (purchasing organizations and company codes) for suppliers between SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition and other applications using SOAP.

Inbound process integration of business-partner relationships

You can exchange business partner relationships between SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition and other applications using SOAP. For this purpose, the new SAP_BX_DCM and SAP_BX_DMU process templates are available. Please note that you can only exchange business partner relationships with systems that support time-dependent business partner relationships. 

Key Benefits

These innovations increase the scope of action for data stewards:

  • through added business content in data models.
  • added support for decentralized maintenance now including relationships.

To stay on track, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

Best,

Markus

entry.title: Develop Innovative AI Apps with SAP BTP Utilizing Generative AI (Webinar)
entry.content:
Are you ready to elevate your business with the power of AI?

The SAP Integration and Certification Center (ICC) is thrilled to invite our valued partners to our webinar that delves into the transformative potential of AI. This webinar is designed to provide you with the knowledge and tools necessary to innovate and grow within the SAP ecosystem utilizing SAP Business AI.

Agenda:

  1. Welcome and Kick-off

    • We’ll start with an introduction to the webinar, outlining the key topics and objectives of the session: guidance how to develop and certify partner solutions on SAP BTP with genAI Hub in SAP AI Core

  2. AI Expert Session

    • Engage with industry-leading AI experts as they explore the latest trends, challenges, and opportunities in AI. Discover how you can leverage SAP Business AI to create smarter, more efficient applications.

  3. Certification for Partner Apps Using Generative AI Hub

    • Learn about SAP Generative AI and how your applications can achieve certification. This session will provide insights into SAP ICC's new BTP-EXT-GENAI certification.

  4. Q&A

    • Have questions? We’ve got answers. Our experts will be available to address any queries you have about AI, certification, or how to maximize your partnership with SAP.

 

Why Attend?

  • Gain Expert Insights: Hear from AI specialists who will share their knowledge and experience in the field.
  • Understand Certification: Learn how to certify your AI-driven applications, giving you a competitive edge.
  • Get Your Questions Answered: Our Q&A session ensures you leave the webinar with all the information you need.

Register Now!

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to stay ahead in the rapidly evolving world of AI. 

SAP PartnerEdge members can [Register Here].

We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Release 2408
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Release 2408

As follow-up to our previous release 2405, this blog post provides an update concerning our most recent release 2408, which is available for customers and partners since August 18th. This particular release features highlights such as a Secondary DB Connections, Wizard for Developer Extensibility, and Integration of SolMan/ChaRM and Cloud TMS.

All available new features can be found on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment page.

ABAP

  • Support for creating custom background processing contexts for asynchronous tasks (details)
  • Support for specifying "Empty Key" as a primary key definition for table types (details)

ABAP Development Tools

  • Support for opening non-native-integrated ABAP development objects in a new fallback editor in ADT, displaying information and read-only source code (details)
  • Support for displaying planned decommissioning dates for deprecated local APIs to help prioritize rework of custom objects (details)
  • Support for hidden values in ABAP Debugger (details)
  • Support for displaying SAP HANA warnings in AMDP Debugger while debugging nested AMDPs (details)
  • Support for exposing APIs via UCL for use in BAS and SAP Build by creating and assigning packages in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (details)

Core Data Services

  • Support for accessing external databases via ABAP CDS and ABAP SQL using CDS external entities and logical external schemas, integrated with SAP HANA federation capabilities (details)
  • Support for creating logical external schemas for outbound SQL access with external entities (details)
  • Support for generating ABAP CDS provider models, including DDIC tables, CDS views, metadata extensions, and service definitions with the Generate ABAP Repository Objects Wizard (details)
  • Support for creating CDS aspects in ABAP CDS syntax to store and reuse field definitions and calculations (details)
  • Support for working with the Core Schema Notation Model object type to import and manage CSN documents (details)
  • Support for importing external CDS models and creating local ABAP CDS data models from them in ABAP Cloud (details)
  • Support for defining reusable measures and filters in CDS for centralized key figures, formulas, and filters, ensuring consistency in complex business reporting
  • Support for improved CDS buffering with buffer propagation, allowing ASQL runtime to read denormalized elements from the ABAP buffer, optimizing memory usage and reducing database joins

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

  • Support for a new RAP BO tools tour in the Feature Explorer to explore RAP BO development features (details)
  • Support for adding parameters to modify runtime parameters in RAP Data Services for print forms, including draft functionality (details)

Annotations

  • Support for using the "ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities" annotation to verify UI use case support, enabling UI projections or direct consumption in a UI service binding (details)
  • Support for using the "Semantics.valueRange" annotation to specify and inform consumers about the value range of a CDS element, applicable to fully ordered data types (details).
  • Support for using the "UI.dataPoint.title" annotation to define the title of a data point (details)

ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC)

  • Support for classic APIs in ATC checks

Custom Code Migration

  • Support for browsing Cloudification Repository content on GitHub (details)
  • Support for adding findings to the baseline in the Custom Code Migration app (details)
  • Support for scheduling custom code analysis as application jobs in the Schedule Custom Code Analysis app (details)

Business Configuration

  • Support for recreating variants in the Custom Business Configurations Fiori app due to a persistence key change (details)
  • Support for selecting "No Transport" in the Business Configuration app and enabling a transport request selection bar in the header toolbar (details)

Reuse Services and Libraries

  • Support for maintaining units and dimensions using released ABAP Cloud APIs (CL_UOM*) without namespace restrictions (details)
  • Support for source templates in DDLX and SQL - Web API bindings in the XCO Library. Longtext reading not supported (details)

UI Services and SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Support for adding a custom logo to the Fiori Launchpad by replacing the SAP logo using the COMPANY_LOGO_URL parameter in the Manage Launchpad Settings app (details)
  • Support for specifying an alternative text for the Home title in the SAP Fiori Launchpad shell bar (details)
  • Support for customizing the browser window and tab title in SAP Fiori Launchpad with additional text options (details)
  • Support for assigning Fiori Space templates to business role templates. Spaces are automatically assigned when creating roles from templates (details)
  • Support for assigning space and page templates to business role templates, with automatic default scoping in test and production systems (details, details)
  • Support for uploading and downloading SAP Fiori Launchpad pages in the Manage Launchpad Pages app, enabling backups and system exchanges (details)

Integration

  • Support for direct push-based integration to enable data exchange between neighboring ABAP Systems via RFC (details)
  • Support for using dynamic topics in Advanced Event Mesh for routing and filtering outbound events, improving efficiency and targeted event delivery (details)
  • Support for retrieving ABAP-based data via SQL service and ODBC driver using CDS SQL-based scalar functions (details)
  • Support for using the Mass Change Wizard with derived business roles (details)
  • Support for displaying IAM apps of all types, including external apps with related authorizations, to manage assigned business roles (details)
  • Support for using new communication targets for HTTP outbound services in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (details)
  • Support for Communication Targets and Logical External Schema in Communication Arrangements
  • Support for assigning APIs to API packages, defining the relationship between packages and API objects (details)
  • Support for creating API packages to hold various types of APIs (details)
  • Support for Communication Management data via OData APIs (details)
  • Support for retrieving support user data via the "Support User – Read Integration" API and corresponding communication scenario (SAP_COM_0347) (details)

Extensibility

  • Support for using the field extension wizard to create extension fields (details)

Administration & Operations

  • Support for monitoring the status of asynchronous emails (details)
  • Support for monitoring HANA CPU usage of custom applications with the HANA Thread Samples app, allowing detailed analysis of top workloads and related SQL statements (details)
  • Support for configuring application server sizes as 0.5 or 2 ACUs in SAP BTP, ABAP environment systems with elastic scaling

 

Additional Information

You can find the product page on SAP BTP, ABAP Environment | SAP Help Portal

For technical documentation, please refer to our documentation.

Please refer to SAP BTP, ABAP environment Roadmap for more details about planned features as part of the product roadmap.

As always, please let us know if you have questions.

entry.title: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 1 - Setup
entry.content:
SAP AI Core is a service in the SAP Business Technology Platform that is designed to handle the execution and operations of your AI assets in a standardized, scalable, and hyperscaler-agnostic way. It provides seamless integration with your SAP solutions. Any AI function can be easily realized using open-source frameworks. SAP AI Core supports full lifecycle management of AI scenarios. Access generative AI capabilities and prompt lifecycle management via the generative AI hub.

The SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) enables developers and technical business users to build multi-channel applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment, and run it natively on mobile devices (Android and iOS) and as a web application (online) in the browser.

In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) to develop mobile applications with AI capabilities.

  • Part 1: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup (Current Blog)
  • Part 2: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Business Use Cases 
  • Part 3: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Measurement Readings (Coming Soon)

 

SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup

1. Subscribe SAP AI Core service and create instance

Please refer to SAP tutorials:

Use Boosters for Free Tier Use of SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad 

for details on how to setup SAP AI Core service and SAP AI Launchpad.

2. Note down the "client-id" and "client-secret" under credentials of your new instance

3. Create new deployment in SAP AI Launchpad and note down the url

4. Configure new destination for SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) Web Client to consume the new AI service deployment

If you want to test and run your app in SAP Business Application Studio (MDK Editor) and deploy it as a web app running on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), you need to configure the BTP destination for your AI service deployment of SAP AI Core services.

an example of destination configuration is as below

#
#Wed Jul 03 03:57:27 UTC 2024
Type=HTTP
clientId=sb-xxxxxxxxx|xsuaa_std\!xxxxx
clientSecret=xxxxxxxxxxxx
HTML5.DynamicDestination=true
Authentication=OAuth2ClientCredentials
URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group=default
Name=AzureOpenAI
tokenServiceURL=https\://test-int.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
URL=https\://api.ai.prod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2/inference/deployments/d123456789c
ProxyType=Internet
tokenServiceURLType=Dedicated
URL.headers.Content-Type=application/json
WebIDEEnabled=true

5. Configure your SAP Mobile Service instance to consume the new AI service deployment

You can create a new mobile service instance or modify your existing instance to add new mobile connectivity for your AI service deployment on SAP AI Core services.

6. Setup your MDK development and testing environment. 

Download and install the SAP Mobile Services Client on your mobile devices, or build your Mobile Development Kit Client using the MDK SDK, and create a new MDK project in Business Application Studio (MDK Editor) as usual.

If you are new to MDK, please check out the following tutorials

Create Your First MDK Application

Build Your Mobile Development Kit Client Using MDK SDK 

After completing the steps mentioned above, our next blog will explore integrating SAP AI Core services into SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) to develop mobile applications with AI capabilities.

Next blog: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 – Business Use Cases

entry.title: Welcome - SAP Auckland Community Events 2024
entry.content:
The local SAP Auckland Community for casual gatherings has kicked off from mid-2024. The initial SAP Inside Track event was hosted at the Lagom Auckland offices, an SAP Partner. We really appreciate those who came to the very first community event to discuss all things #SAP #BTP related.

Special thanks to our speakers Murali Shanmugham, John Paul Agcamaran and Martin Coetzee. All three shared some great insights from Sapphire, through to a recent CAP Course - complete with a demo of the new AI assistant Joule - and the importance of understanding GROW and RISE and what that means for you on your BP journey.

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/activity-7211476493279846401-o4xh?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

The 2nd Auckland SAP Inside Track event is being held a day before the local Annual SAP User Group and Summit in New Zealand. This will be hosted at the Deloitte offices, another SAP Partner. 

We look forward to seeing this community grow and for the continuous variety to speakers and topics - as well as some serious socialising!

entry.title: ALM Nuggets: Creation of Public Views on SAP BTP for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Introduction

With this blogpost I will describe the new possibility to create public views, which can be used by all users within your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Views

You find views in mostly every Apps in SAP Cloud ALM on the top left side.

Steps on the SAP BTP Cockpit

Open your SAP BTP cockpit:
https://cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Open the subaccount that contains your SAP Cloud ALM tenant:
Choose Security  > Role Collections > Create 

Name: Public View Editor
Description: Create and edit public views, which are shared with all users in your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Under Roles, select the role FlexPublicViewEditor and choose Add and click on Save.

 

Under Users, select all users who should be able to create public views and click on Save.

SAP Cloud ALM

It is now possible to create public views.
And every SAP Cloud ALM user can use the public views.

In the user management of SAP Cloud ALM you can now also see the new role:

That's it.

entry.title: ALM Nuggets: Erstellen öffentlicher Ansichten auf der SAP BTP für SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Einführung

In diesem Blogbeitrag beschreibe ich die neue Möglichkeit, öffentliche Ansichten zu erstellen, die von allen Benutzern in Ihrem SAP Cloud ALM tenant verwendet werden können.

Ansichten

Sie finden Ansichten in fast allen Apps in SAP Cloud ALM oben links.

Schritte im SAP BTP Cockpit

Öffnen Sie das SAP BTP Cockpit:
https://cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Öffnen Sie den Subaccount für Ihren SAP Cloud ALM tenant:
Wählen Sie: Security  > Role Collections > Create 

Name: Public View Editor
Description: Create and edit public views, which are shared with all users in your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Unter Roles, selektieren Sie die Rolle FlexPublicViewEditor und fügen diese hinzu.

Unter Users wählen Sie diejenigen Benutzer aus welche öffentliche Ansichten erstellen dürfen.

SAP Cloud ALM

Es ist jetzt möglich, öffentliche Ansichten zu erstellen.
Und jeder SAP Cloud ALM Benutzer kann die öffentlichen Ansichten verwenden.

Im User Management von SAP Cloud ALM können sie die neue Rolle nun ebenfalls sehen:

Das wars, vielen Dank fürs lesen.

entry.title: XSA Service Instances Migration from On-premise to Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
In the last blog, you learned migration from an on-premise SAP HANA database to an SAP HANA Cloud database instance using the Self-Service Migration for SAP HANA Cloud. Now, will see on how to migrate the XS advanced service instances from on-premise to Cloud Foundry.

Figure: Migration Landscape

Pre-requisites

  • Connectivity Service Entitlement with the service plan “connectivity_proxy” must be enabled for your subaccount using the SAP BTP cockpit.
  • Supported for SAP HANA Database Revision 53 or greater.
  • Source and Tenant databases has XS advanced installed.
  • Migration user in the XSA tenant database of source system with necessary privileges.
  • Establish a connection to the XSA tenant database of source system using Cloud Connector.

Note: Services Instances in default SAP space is not supported for migration.

Procedure

  1. Open SAP HANA Cloud Central, navigate to “Migrations”.
  2. Choose “Create Migration”, specify the migration information and then “Create”.
  3. In the PLAN phase,
  • Enter the virtual host and port that you specified in the cloud connector configuration.
  • Enable Cloud Connector and specify the Cloud Connector Location ID.
  • Enter the credentials of the migration user that was created in the XSA tenant database of the source system.
  • Check Compatibility with SAP HANA Cloud.

  • Tool runs a series of database and application compatibility checks. Once the Self-Service Migration tool finishes checking your database, it lists any incompatibilities found. 
  • Only checks with the severity 'CRITICAL' and 'UNKNOWN' will forbid the migration. Checks with the severity 'ERROR' or below will allow the migration; however, it is possible that some functionality may not work as expected in the target database.
  • Review and resolve the incompatibilities where possible. Choose Proceed to Preparation Phase.

    4. In the PREPARATION phase, provide the following information:

       Migrate XS advanced service instances (SAP HANA Schemas and HDI containers) to Cloud Foundry.

     As the source database has XS advanced installed and the tenant database selected for migration is enabled for XS advanced, the connection to the XS advanced database is configured through the SAP Cloud Connector.

  • Provide the login credentials of the XS advanced administrator user.
  • Configure the connection to the XS advanced runtime through Cloud Connector.
  • Choose Configure Endpoint to generate a configuration file that you  have to upload to SAP Cloud Connector in a later step.

  • Download the Configuration file and upload the same in Cloud Connector. Refer to point 9 in the link for more details.

  • Click on Save and Proceed.

  • Specify the credentials of the SAP BTP technical user.

  • Map the source and target spaces which contain the service instances to be migrated.

  • Specify the credentials of the migration user and Choose Save and Proceed to Select Target.

  • Choose the target database for the migration and enter the credentials of the DBADMIN user on the target database.

  • Confirm all the migration choices and Choose Continue to Execution Phase to proceed to the next phase of the migration.

     5. In the EXECUTION phase, choose “Start Migration” to start the migration process and monitor progress in the status bars. After the migration, a summary is displayed, and you can proceed to the VALIDATION phase.

    6. In the VALIDATION phase, the metadata and data of the migrated content are checked for consistency, also the record count of the tables to be migrated can be checked. Choose “Proceed to Finalization Phase”.

    7. In the FINALIZATION phase, the finalization process removes all artifacts that were temporarily stored by the migration service.Choose “Deactivate the migration user” and “Finalize Migration” to complete the migration and start the post-migration clean-up.

   8. The XS service instances is migrated to Cloud Foundry. A summary screen is displayed, containing information about the completed migration, including statistics about the migrated database, migration step durations, and validation statuses.

   9. “Clean Up Process” will archive the completed migration. Once the migration is archived, it serves as a reference and cannot be altered. Up to 20 migrations can be archived.

 

Hope this blog has helped! For more information refer SAP documentation SAP HANA Cloud Migration Guide.

entry.title: Joule and SAP Business AI Now available for RISE with SAP Customers
entry.content:
SAP’s vision for Business AI is to bring powerful Generative AI capabilities across all our products and services through SAP BTP to benefit the customers in achieving measurable business outcomes. As you are already aware, SAP introduced Joule in Sep 2023, a copilot that truly understands your business revolutionizing the interactions with SAP systems making every touchpoint count and every task simpler in a secure and compliant way. Joule will be embedded across SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio, delivering proactive and contextualized insights from SAP solutions and third-party sources. Joule is everywhere, was one of the most discussed topics at SAPPHIRE 2024, and needs no introduction, find out everything you need to know about Joule on this blog.

We are excited to announce Joule is now available for RISE with SAP customers with significant enhancements to RISE with SAP commercial packages focused on accelerating digital transformation and bringing the power of AI to all our customers. Joule, our AI copilot, will now be available for all existing and new RISE with SAP customers, natively integrating into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Since launching RISE with SAP in Jan 2021, our mission to transform customers into a cloud operating model had a strong momentum in RISE adoption. We have helped over 6000+ customers across various industries and geographies to transform to a cloud operating model through RISE with SAP among which 3000+ customers went live.

During this journey of cloud transformation, we have observed that it is essential for every enterprise to evolve with the latest technologies in order to adopt them and remain competitive in the market. To maintain the pace with the need for agility and innovation, while moving towards fast-changing technologies, customers need to adapt to changes that are evolving in AI technologies and innovations. As AI and Generative AI have become the top priorities for every enterprise striving to stay competitive and maintain its market position, there are a few challenges that all enterprises must acknowledge and overcome.

Below are the transformational drivers that are impacting customers to be prepared for the future.

  • Rising Customer Expectations: In the digital age, customers are more empowered than ever, driving the demand for exceptional experiences, personalized services, and seamless interactions across various channels.
  • Technological Advancements: The rapid emergence of technologies like generative AI presents both a challenge and an opportunity for businesses to stay ahead by leveraging innovation for growth.
  • Operational Efficiency: Enhancing operational efficiency is crucial for enterprises aiming to streamline processes, reduce costs, and boost productivity.
  • Competitive Pressure: To maintain a competitive edge and secure market position, companies must continuously evolve and adapt to the ever-changing landscape.

To address these challenges, SAP Cloud ERP is infused with AI capabilities to accelerate business ability and meet business goals.

McKinsey reports that Generative AI could generate over $2.6 to $4.4 trillion incremental value annually to the global market. As AI adoption accelerates, particularly in the area of Generative AI, there is an extreme need to capitalize on this technology. Leaders across industries are prioritizing investments and closely tracking the potential impact of Generative AI, recognizing that failure to stay competitive could pose significant risks. CEOs are making these strategic decisions to avoid losing market share and maintaining their competitive edge.

 

Why only cloud? And limitations of On-Premise:

The latest technological advancements like Generative AI need high computation power for efficient and accelerated performance. It requires a level of infrastructure that can handle the speed at which the innovations are changing and, run, activate, and deploy the AI use cases effectively. Data plays a critical role in implementing AI use cases, with the volume of data growing rapidly each day. To efficiently process and fine-tune this data for optimal and accelerated performance, the latest and most powerful infrastructure that can scale according to evolving requirements is essential.

The on-premise environments become challenging when scaling the systems to meet increased AI demand. The computation power for on-premise systems becomes costly and time-consuming. Significant resources and infrastructure are required to grow and maintain hardware and software systems. The on-premise environment presents significant challenges, making a cloud a more effective option considering scalability, speed, and cost-effectiveness.

Benefits to Customers 

By adopting AI, customers can gain benefits that can impact both their Business and People. In the field of business, AI enhances decision-making faster, with data-driven insights and recommendations, ensuring safer and more informed choices. For individuals, AI transforms work through automation and streamlines user experiences, ultimately driving better business outcomes

Benefits of AI for Business:

Reinvent Customer Service with personalized experiences that address customer needs quickly.

Boost Finance Excellence with AI that helps optimize working capital and closing functions.

Drive Operational Excellence by optimizing manufacturing and asset performance and elevating product quality.

Advance Intelligent Order Fulfillment by streamlining and automating the order fulfillment process.

Benefits of AI for People:

Empower End Users to increase efficiency and eliminate redundant tasks ​

Interact with Business using natural language for informational, navigational, and transactional assistance.​

Uncover Insights that would otherwise be challenging or time-consuming to discover 

 

SAP Business AI in RISE with SAP

Aligning with SAP’s Business AI strategy, SAP launched the integration of Joule starting with Success Factors in Dec 2023. Now, Joule is available for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud for RISE with SAP customers. Joule offers a navigational pattern helping in navigating to relevant SAP Fiori apps and letting the user work directly in the app’s web UI for new and occasional users without prior knowledge of individual SAP Fiori apps.

RISE customers will have the advantage of experiencing generative AI capabilities across SAP cloud ERP. All existing and new RISE customers will have access to Joule to quickly access apps, effectively gain insights on business objects, and speedily resolve user issues. There are a few prerequisites to access Joule on SAP S/4HANA quoted below:

Prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP S/4HANA (requires administrator role):

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01 or later.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform, with the following entitlements
    • Joule Entitlements - Joule(das-application), please refer to license topic
    • SAP Workzone, standard edition
    • SAP Cloud Connectivity
    • Cloud Foundry
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • SAP Cloud Connector with Principal Propagation
    • System Mapping to expose the required HTTP protocol

To know more about the capabilities of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition and complete the configuration guide follow this link.

Commercial Packages

As part of RISE with SAP offerings, entitlements are provided with different customer profile packages like Base Edition, Premium Edition, and Premium Plus Edition. Joule is accessible in all available packages Base, Premium, and Premium Plus for RISE customers. Joule will be accessible by all existing and new RISE customers fulfilling the prerequisites mentioned above to use capabilities. Joule and Generative AI will be part of the new SKU as entitlements under the Artificial Intelligence domain.

RISE Base Edition: Provides small organizations with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition functionalities with a lean setup

RISE Premium Edition: Enables organization’s business transformation supported by built-in AI capabilities into SAP applications, process automation, low-code business application development, and access to supplier networks.

RISE Premium Plus Edition: Extends premium by unlocking more AI scenarios, including sustainability solutions, and advanced finance capabilities.

AI has significant potential to accelerate business processes and optimize decision-making. Since AI is at the top of every enterprise’s priority list, we are constantly innovating to deliver more AI-powered business processes and bringing the capabilities of Business AI as an entitlement as part of the SKU for RISE with SAP. Generative AI capabilities are available for RISE Premium and RISE Premium Plus packages where respective AI units are available based on the package. RISE with SAP Premium customers are provided with AI units to pilot AI-driven innovations like SAP Cash Application, SAP Business Integrity Screening, and SAP Service Management.

SAP AI Unit is a single SKU. SAP’s AI use cases are commercialized as capabilities within a single SKU regardless of LoBs. All customers can license SAP AI Unit to purchase AI units but to be able to use specific AI capabilities, you need to fulfill the licensing pre-requisites.

AI units are like a virtual currency that allows you to consume a wide set of AI use cases across different LoB solutions within SAP which included Joule overages. AI units are based on annual subscriptions and are sold in a block of 100 AI units. The minimum block to purchase is 1 per year and must be used by the end of the year as all unused AI units will be lost if not consumed by the end of the year.

Commercials & Estimator

RISE customers will have entitlements of 2500 Joule messages per FUE per contract year free of charge to interact with SAP A/4HANA Cloud, private edition. Once the entitled number of messages is consumed and you are willing to continue accessing Joule, you can purchase additional messages with AI units SKU. A message is an electronic communication exchanged via the capability of cloud service. For cloud services like Joule, a user request, together with the corresponding response from Joule is considered as 1 message.

Customers can use the AI Unit Estimator to estimate x number of AI units required to run the selected AI capabilities and can request a quote from the estimator directly. After selecting the AI feature, the customer can add the number of transactions (in a block of 10,000) you would need for the selected AI feature. Cost estimation is automatically shown based on the selection and a quote can be created from the same page for the selected AI feature and the transactions based on the requirement.

Another way where customers can access the AI services is from the SAP for Me dashboard under the Portfolio & Products tab by selecting Business AI and activating the AI feature. The features are listed based on the SAP products they belong to, where customers can select whether to activate the feature or not. The customer has the option to view the consumption for the respective feature or calculate cost estimation for the feature by selecting an estimator for the respective feature.

Early Adopter Care (EAC) Program: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

SAP offers innovation adopter services to help you jump-start your projects with SAP’s latest products and solutions. SAP Early Adopter Care connects customers with products to help them simplify, innovate, and digitize fast. Through the support of the Early Adopter Care project coach, we enable two-way communication, and transparency, and allow stakeholders to minimize project risk.

Benefits of the EAC program:

Support with early implementation of SAP’s latest innovations.

Direct channel to development for product and solution insights.

To join the Early adopter care program for Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition, check out this link. Registration for the EAC program for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition started on 15th July and is open till 31st Dec 2024.

Conditions for participation:

Participation in the early adopter care program is voluntary, at the sole discretion of SAP, and dependent on the following requisites.

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01
  • Plans to use Joule in production
  • Willingness to become a reference
  • The full set of restrictions can be found here General Constraints for Joule & Constraints for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Customer Engagement Initiative: AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you, as an SAP customer or partner, to get insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

What is in for you?

  • Opportunity to define and shape the product's future.
  • Gain early insights and actively engage to learn and shape the new innovations that matter.
  • Access and engage directly with the product engineering teams.

How to get engaged with us?

Visit this link and register your company for this opportunity.

 

Joule Integration – Step-by-step guide

To configure Joule and integrate with SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, check the below blogs for a step-by-step guide.

Joule – Getting Started with Joule and SAP SuccessFactors

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

entry.title: Unifying Your Business Operations: Integrating SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Business One
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) need more than just robust ERP systems to stay competitive. they need integrated digital workspaces that unify access to all their critical applications. SAP Build Work Zone, when integrated with SAP Business One, offers a powerful solution to streamline operations, enhance collaboration, and boost productivity. This blog post provides an overview of SAP Build Work Zone and its use cases for SAP Business customers, as well as the overview of this integration, concluding with recommended e-learning resources for those interested in exploring the details further.

 

SAP Build Work Zone Overview

SAP Build Work Zone is a digital workplace solution that offers a unified, role-based user experience, integrating applications, content, and processes in a single interface. It comes in two editions:

 

SAP Build Work Zone Use cases

For SAP Business One customers, where often only about 10% of employees use the ERP system, there's an opportunity for partners to extend their reach by targeting the non-ERP users and even non-Employee users with Work Zone solutions. Traditionally, these users have relied on third-party or custom-built solutions. In addition, SAP Work Zone Standard Edtion can complement other BTP apps and services, particularly since it is included at no additional cost with Build Apps and Build Process Automation.

The Advanced Edition is also a cost-effective option for replacing third-party groupware or intranet/extranet solutions.

This approach enables partners to generate recurring cloud revenue and additional income from contents and services.

 

Integration with SAP Business One

This illustrates the Build Work Zone demo site, featuring tiles and cards.

  • HR Space includes the Employee Self-Service page with two tile apps:
    • Employee Lookup app - built with SAP Build Apps
    • Employee Store Apps - 3rd party UI5 app
  • Sales Space contains two pages:
    • Overview Page features ...
      • Company dashboard - brought from SAP Analytics Cloud Story
      • Business Partners app - built with SAP Build Apps
    • Sales Processing Page is divided into two sections “Sales Orders” and “Invoicing”, each hosting ...
      • Business One Web Client URL app - opens SAP Business One Web Client Views
      • UI Integration card - built with SAP Business Application Studio with no-code manner. it displays the most-concise pieces of business data and enable straightforward and decisive user action in a limited-space container, helping users structure their work in an intuitive and dynamic way.

 

This site was configured with the view mode "Space and Pages – New Experience" to host UI integration cards. 

 

Then how to create a tile and card in a site? Here are three ways to manage business contents in a site.

 

Overview of Step-by-Step Process to create the above demo site in Build Work Zone standard edition, integrated with SAP Business One

Prerequisites

  1. Maintain a destination to represent SAP Business One service layer in BTP subaccount.
  2. Configure SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication (IAS) to let IAS users work as business users across the different systems with single sign-on. 
  3. Optionally create applications in the SAP IAS admin console for SAP Business One if you want to achieve single sign-on with SAP Business One Web Client.

Configuration steps

  1. Create the Work Zone standard edition in BTP subaccount.
  2. Prepare for apps for Tiles and Cards for the above demo site.
    • Create applications in Build Apps and/or Business Application Studio and deploy them to HTML5 repository (the managed router created by Build Work Zone) in the BTP subaccount.
    • Create UI integration cards and build a content package for UI integration cards in Business Application Studio.
    • Create a SAP Analytics Cloud Story.
  3. Create a new site in the Build Work Zone Site Manager
  4. Create the 1st space for HR 
  5. Create the 2nd space for sales and its 1st page
  6. Deploy the content package for the UI integration cards to Work Zone and assign the role
  7. Create the 2nd page for the sales space, including UI integration cards

 

Conclusion and Call-to-action

Integrating SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Business One provides a powerful solution for SMEs looking to unify their IT environments, enhance productivity, and improve business agility. With its flexible capabilities, role-based access, and mobile readiness, SAP Build Work Zone is an essential tool for businesses aiming to streamline their operations and empower their employees and business partners.

For the detailed step-by-step guides on how to implement this integration and explore its full potential, access the e-learning course on the SAP PartnerEdge portal (S-user required) : 

  • Presentation
  • Step-by-step video
  • How to create UI Integration Cards exercise
